title: circaea lutetiana var. ericetorum
intro: Circaea lutetiana var. ericetorum, commonly known as Wood Sorrel, is a rare and little-known variant of the Circaea lutetiana plant species that is native to Europe and is primarily found in heathland and moorland areas. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Circaea lutetiana var. ericetorum include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system, making it a valuable herb for treating various ailments. Herbal preparations of Wood Sorrel can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are often used in combination with other herbs to enhance their medicinal properties.
